name: Apple Foundation Model 3 Core (on-device, ~3B)
archDisplay: Dense on-device transformer, third generation of Apple's on-device model line
note: Third-generation on-device model announced at WWDC26 (2026-06-08), a quality step up from the 2025 on-device model; gains image-input understanding via the Foundation Models framework's new multimodal update.
verificationNotes:
Confirmed via Apple's own machinelearning.apple.com research post, 9to5Mac's WWDC26 explainer, and MacRumors' June 9, 2026 coverage of the Platforms State of the Union. ctxK=32 is CARRIED FORWARD from the prior-generation on-device model (Apple did not publish a new context-window figure for this generation in the sources found) — flagged as an estimate, not a confirmed new number. No standard-benchmark (MMLU/GPQA-style) numeric scores were published for this generation — Apple reported only internal human-preference win-rates (e.g. 45.6% vs 23.3% preferred over the 2025 model on text tasks), which are not on the fixed benchmark-slug list, so no 'results' rows are included rather than mis-mapping them. This is a genuine gap: the corpus's Apple folder only has the original ~3B on-device model from June 2024. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): No new benchmark results found for Apple Foundation Model 3 Core (on-device, ~3B) — corpus file corpus/models/apple/apple-foundation-model-3-core-on-device-3b.json remains at results: [].

Search methodology (widened per instructions): Apple is a US vendor, so Chinese/Japanese-source search angles (point 1/2 of the brief) don't apply. Focused on point 3 instead:
- Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai): no mention of any Apple Foundation Model in leaderboards or model pages — Apple's on-device models aren't API-accessible outside Apple's own devices/frameworks, so AA has no way to benchmark them.
- LMArena/LMSYS: no Apple Foundation Model entries; the July-2026 leaderboard is dominated by Claude/GPT/Gemini frontier models, and edge/on-device coverage there is Qwen/Gemma, not Apple.
- OpenRouter/HuggingFace: no AFM-3 model card or listing (the model isn't distributed as open weights or via API).
- Direct fetch of Apple's own launch post (machinelearning.apple.com/research/introducing-third-generation-of-apple-foundation-models): confirmed again it reports ONLY human-preference win-rates (e.g., 45.6% vs 23.3% preferred over 2025 baseline; TTS MOS scores; 61% image-understanding preference) — explicitly no MMLU/GPQA/IFEval/SWE-bench numbers for the 3rd-gen models.
- Checked machinelearning.apple.com/updates/ for any newer Apple research post (June–July 2026) that might contain the promised follow-up tech report — none found yet; multiple secondary sources (ofox.ai developer read, 9to5Mac) note Apple said it would publish "updated evaluations and benchmarks" in a technical report "later this summer" (2026), which as of today (2026-07-15) does not appear to have been released yet.
- Found and ruled out a false lead: an AppleLeaker tweet and several SEO-farm articles cite "AFM on-device: MMLU 67.8, IFEval 85.1, MMMLU 60.60, MGSM 74.91, beats Qwen-2.5-3B/Gemma-3-4B/Gemma-3n-E4B." Traced this to arXiv 2507.13575 ("Apple Intelligence Foundation Language Models: Tech Report 2025", published July 2025) and confirmed via Twitter snowflake-ID decoding that the tweet itself dates to ~July 2025 — these numbers describe the PREVIOUS (2024/2025) on-device AFM generation, not the June-2026 third-generation AFM 3 Core that is the subject of this file. Did not apply them to avoid mis-attribution.
- Wayback Machine: not separately queried because Apple's launch page is live and current (not a dead page needing archival recovery); the live page was already fetched directly and is the authoritative source.

Conclusion: this remains a genuine, expected gap — Apple has not yet published any standard fixed-benchmark-slug numeric results for the 3rd-generation on-device model as of 2026-07-15. The promised follow-up technical report (which historically is where Apple publishes MMLU/MGSM/IFEval-style numbers, per the 2025 precedent at arXiv 2507.13575) has apparently not shipped yet. Recommend re-checking machinelearning.apple.com/research/ periodically over the coming weeks/months, since that report — once published — is very likely to contain benchmark-slug-mappable results (MMLU, MGSM, IFEval, MMMLU are the exact metrics Apple used last cycle). ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): Checked the two specified structured sources for Apple Foundation Model 3 Core (on-device, ~3B) — corpus file corpus/models/apple/apple-foundation-model-3-core-on-device-3b.json — and found no results in either.

1. Epoch AI raw benchmark data feed:
   - Fetched https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json (179KB) directly. This file is benchmark-definition metadata only (title, category, description, methodology per benchmark slug like gpqa-diamond, frontiermath-tier-4-v1, etc.) — it contains no per-model scores, so searched it anyway for "apple"/"afm"/"foundation model" strings: zero matches.
   - Fetched https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv (2.3MB, 5981 rows) — this is the actual per-model-run results feed (columns: model, Organization, Hugging Face developer id, benchmark, score, etc.). Parsed with Python csv module and checked all three identifying columns across all 999 unique model entri... [truncated; see round13_result.json for full text]

[ROUND 20 — HF-raw-content + academic-citation search, 2026-07-16] No new benchmark results found for Apple Foundation Model 3 Core (on-device, ~3B) via either of the two specified source types. Both were genuinely attempted and exhausted; nothing was found to add.

1. HuggingFace model card check — NO REPO EXISTS for this model.
- Fetched https://huggingface.co/apple directly and listed its full model catalog: SimpleSD collection (SimpleSD-4B-thinking/instruct, SimpleSD-30B-instruct), CLaRa collection (7B-Instruct/Base/E2E), CADD-Base-7B, DiffuCoder-7B-Base, OpenELM (Base/Instruct), FastVLM, MobileCLIP 2 (S3/S4), DepthPro, Sharp, FastViT Core ML, DETR ResNet50 Core ML, and a "Core ML Gallery Models" collection. None of these is Apple Foundation Model 3 Core, AFM-3, or any on-device chat/instruct LLM matching this corpus entry — Apple's HF org only hosts older research releases (OpenELM) and vision/perception models, not the proprietary on-device AFM line, which is distributed exclusively through the closed Foundation Models framework on-device, not as downloadable weights.
- Searched for direct repo-name guesses (apple/AFM-3, afm-3-core, etc.) — none exist. The only "AFM" hits on Hugging Face belong to a completely unrelated model, arcee-ai/AFM-4.5B ("Arcee Foundation Model"), from a different vendor (Arcee.ai) — confirmed as a false-lead/naming collision and not used.
- No eval_results.json or "Files and versions" eval artifacts exist because there is no repo to check.
- Conclusion: this model has no HuggingFace presence at all (expected, since Apple ships AFM-3 Core only via on-device inference in the Foundation Models framework, never as open/downloadable weights or an API-hosted card).

2. Academic citation search (Semantic Scholar + Google Scholar) — NO CITING PAPERS FOUND, and the model has no indexable paper of its own yet.
- Apple has NOT published an arXiv/technical-report paper for the 3rd-generation AFM (announced 2026-06-08, ~5 weeks before this search on 2026-07-16). The only Apple Foundation Models arXiv paper is 2507.13575 ("Apple Intelligence Foundation Language Models: Tech Report 2025"), confirmed via direct fetch of arxiv.org/abs/2507.13575 to be at v3 (dated 2025-08-27) and to cover ONLY the prior (2024/2025) on-device 3B + server MoE generation — it does not mention "AFM 3 Core" or the 2026 third generation at all. This reconfirms round-7's finding that the AppleLeaker/SEO-farm MMLU=67.8/IFEval=85.1/MGSM=74.91 numbers trace to this older paper and generation, not to the file's subject model.
- Since AFM-3 Core has no citable paper of its own, there is nothing for other papers to "cite and report benchmark scores for" in a comparison table — this citation-search pattern (e.g., the ERNIE-3.0-Titan case) structurally requires the target model to already have an indexed paper, which AFM-3 Core does not.
- Semantic Scholar Graph API (api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search) was queried with multiple query variants ("Apple Foundation Model 3 Core on-device", "Apple Foundation Model on-device 2026", "Apple on-device foundation model AFM") — every attempt returned HTTP 429 "Too Many Requests" from the shared sandbox egress IP, even after three separate delays/retries. This is an infrastructure rate-limit, not a null result; if a Semantic Scholar API key becomes available in a future round, worth retrying with authenticated requests for higher rate limits.
- As a fallback, fetched semanticscholar.org's HTML search UI directly (JS-rendered, returned only a loading shell with no results in the static fetch) and ran Google-Scholar-scoped web searches for "AFM 3 Core" / "Apple Foundation Model 3" — all returned only the same vendor/press coverage (Apple's own ML research post, 9to5Mac, MacStories, WisGate, letsdatascience, ofox.ai) already cataloged in prior rounds, with zero academic/citing papers surfaced.
- Conclusion: this is a genuine, expected gap given the model's age (~5 weeks old at check time) — no academic paper exists yet either for or citing AFM-3 Core, so this source type cannot currently surface results. Recommend re-trying the Semantic Scholar citation-search angle in a later round once (a) Apple's promised follow-up technical report ships (per round-7's finding that Apple said benchmarks are coming "later this summer" 2026), which would itself become a citable/searchable paper, and (b) enough time has passed for downstream NLP papers to cite it in comparison tables.

Net result: results remain [] for this model. Both specified source types were exhausted this round with negative-but-informative findings (no HF repo exists; no paper exists yet to be cited). No fabricated or inferred numbers were applied.

[ROUND 21 — light delta-check, 2026-07-16] Re-checked Apple's own launch post (machinelearning.apple.com/research/introducing-third-generation-of-apple-foundation-models) via direct fetch plus fresh web searches for "Apple Foundation Model 3 Core benchmark technical report July 2026" and tech-report-specific terms: no change since round 20 (also dated 2026-07-16). The page still reports only human-preference win-rates (e.g., 45.6% vs 23.3% for AFM 3 Core on text tasks) and MOS scores, and still ends with the same promise to publish "updated evaluations and benchmarks in a technical report later this summer" — that report has not shipped yet. No new MMLU/GPQA/HumanEval/etc. numbers exist for this generation as of this check. results remains [].
